# v13: log the session-summary backlog count on the orchestrator periodic sweep (parity with the mini-summary count)

## Problem (v13 observability)

#12810 added the **mini-summary** backlog count to the orchestrator log: the `memory-summary-backfill` task only fires when there's a backlog, so it always logs `pending-memory-minisummary:N`. The **session-summary** side has no equivalent on its dominant path:

- The `summary` task's trigger (`ai/daemons/orchestrator/scheduling/summary.mjs`) logs `pending-summarization:N` **only** on the `pending-summarization` path (explicit `SummarizationJobs` markers).
- When there are no markers, it fires via **`periodic-sweep`** with reason `periodic-sweep:600000` — **no backlog count**. The periodic sweep is exactly the path that handles "ordinary unsummarized sessions," so the real session-summary backlog depth is invisible.

Net: we can see the mini-summary backlog drain but are blind to the session-summary backlog (the operator's "we need both").

## Fix

- Add `getPendingSessionSummaryCount(db)` to `summary.mjs`: a fail-soft graph-direct count of **sessions with `AGENT_MEMORY` rows but no `SESSION_SUMMARY`** (the real unsummarized-session backlog — parallel to the mini-summary count's "rows missing a miniSummary" semantics, not the capped `SummarizationJobs` marker fetch).
- Thread it into `buildSummaryTrigger`'s `periodic-sweep` reason so every periodic sweep logs the depth, e.g. `periodic-sweep:600000 pending-session-summary:N`. Wire the count fn in `getDueTask` (test seam, like #12810).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The `summary` periodic-sweep trigger reason includes the uncapped unsummarized-session count.
- [ ] `getPendingSessionSummaryCount` is fail-soft (returns `null` when the table/graph is unavailable) and counts distinct `AGENT_MEMORY` sessionIds lacking a `SESSION_SUMMARY`.
- [ ] Deterministic scheduling tests (pure-function, no live graph): periodic-sweep reason carries the count; fail-soft path.
- [ ] Output shape otherwise unchanged (additive — backward-compatible, like #12810).

## Relations

Parallel to #12810 (mini-summary backlog count). Relates to #12819 / #12820 (session-summary silent-gap fix), #12817 LEAF 1.

Authored by Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code)

- 2026-06-09T11:58:45Z @neo-claude-opus referenced in commit `dd868a1` - "feat(orchestrator): log the session-summary backlog count on the periodic sweep (#12821)

The mini-summary backfill task only fires when there is a backlog, so it always logs
pending-memory-minisummary:N (#12810). The summary task also fires on the periodic sweep, whose
reason was bare periodic-sweep:600000 — so the session-summary backlog depth was invisible on
its dominant path.

- new getPendingSessionSummaryCount(db): fail-soft graph-direct count of sessions with
  AGENT_MEMORY turns but no SESSION_SUMMARY (the real unsummarized-session backlog, not the
  capped SummarizationJobs marker fetch).
- buildSummaryTrigger appends pending-session-summary:N to the periodic-sweep reason when the
  count is available; reason otherwise unchanged (backward-compatible).
- getDueTask computes the count only when the periodic sweep will fire (no handover, no marked
  jobs, interval due) so the COUNT query runs at most once per sweep, never per poll.
- +4 deterministic scheduling tests; existing reason shape preserved when no count is provided.

Parallel to #12810 (mini-summary backlog count). Relates to #12819 / #12820, #12817 LEAF 1."
- 2026-06-09T12:17:21Z @neo-claude-opus referenced in commit `be799f7` - "fix(orchestrator): null-safe anti-join in getPendingSessionSummaryCount (#12821)

Addresses the cross-family review: the unsummarized-session count used NOT IN (subquery), which
is not null-safe — a single SESSION_SUMMARY row with a NULL sessionId makes NOT IN evaluate to
NULL for every row, silently collapsing the backlog count to 0. Switched to a correlated
NOT EXISTS, which is immune to the NULL trap.

- new real-graph spec (SessionSummaryBacklogCount.spec.mjs, :memory: under UNIT_TEST_MODE,
  CI-safe — no Chroma/live model): proves the count reports unsummarized sessions and that a
  NULL-sessionId SESSION_SUMMARY does not collapse it; plus fail-soft.

18/18 scheduling specs green."
- 2026-06-09T12:35:55Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-06-09T12:35:55Z @tobiu referenced in commit `6a6b0bf` - "feat(orchestrator): log the session-summary backlog count on the periodic sweep (#12821) (#12822)

* feat(orchestrator): log the session-summary backlog count on the periodic sweep (#12821)

The mini-summary backfill task only fires when there is a backlog, so it always logs
pending-memory-minisummary:N (#12810). The summary task also fires on the periodic sweep, whose
reason was bare periodic-sweep:600000 — so the session-summary backlog depth was invisible on
its dominant path.

- new getPendingSessionSummaryCount(db): fail-soft graph-direct count of sessions with
  AGENT_MEMORY turns but no SESSION_SUMMARY (the real unsummarized-session backlog, not the
  capped SummarizationJobs marker fetch).
- buildSummaryTrigger appends pending-session-summary:N to the periodic-sweep reason when the
  count is available; reason otherwise unchanged (backward-compatible).
- getDueTask computes the count only when the periodic sweep will fire (no handover, no marked
  jobs, interval due) so the COUNT query runs at most once per sweep, never per poll.
- +4 deterministic scheduling tests; existing reason shape preserved when no count is provided.

Parallel to #12810 (mini-summary backlog count). Relates to #12819 / #12820, #12817 LEAF 1.

* fix(orchestrator): null-safe anti-join in getPendingSessionSummaryCount (#12821)

Addresses the cross-family review: the unsummarized-session count used NOT IN (subquery), which
is not null-safe — a single SESSION_SUMMARY row with a NULL sessionId makes NOT IN evaluate to
NULL for every row, silently collapsing the backlog count to 0. Switched to a correlated
NOT EXISTS, which is immune to the NULL trap.

- new real-graph spec (SessionSummaryBacklogCount.spec.mjs, :memory: under UNIT_TEST_MODE,
  CI-safe — no Chroma/live model): proves the count reports unsummarized sessions and that a
  NULL-sessionId SESSION_SUMMARY does not collapse it; plus fail-soft.

18/18 scheduling specs green."
